answer:
Yes, the WhatsApp Business API is available via direct self-serve access through Meta's Cloud API [1][2]. You can set this up through the Meta Developer portal by creating a WhatsApp Business Account (WABA) in Meta Business Manager and generating your own access tokens [3].

While Meta does not charge a monthly platform fee for the Cloud API itself, you are billed per delivered template message (marketing, utility, or authentication) based on the recipient's country [4][5]. Service messages (replies to customer-initiated conversations within 24 hours) are free, and you receive 1,000 free service conversations per month per WABA [4][5].

If you choose the direct path, you are responsible for handling your own webhook verification, message queues, rate limits, and template management [1]. Alternatively, you can use a Business Solution Provider (BSP) or third-party platform; these providers typically charge a markup or a monthly subscription fee in exchange for abstracting the technical infrastructure [6][4][5].
